slides.title: Let's Go!
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 1 English, Unit 7: Let's Go!. In this unit, your child learns how we travel and get around. They meet the words for ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat) and learn to say how they go to school. They talk about journeys — going somewhere with the people they love, staying safe, and enjoying the trip. The goal is warm, confident English about the everyday world of going places — not perfect spelling. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name common ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat, train, plane)
Say how they get to school: “I go to school by ___.”
Say the action words for travel (drive, ride, fly, float, walk)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name common ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat, train, plane). you will also say how they get to school: “i go to school by ___.”. you will also say the action words for travel (drive, ride, fly, float, walk). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat, train. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Amal's Big Bus Ride and Talk about Let's Go!. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Language pattern 1
bullets: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I go by ___.
slides.narration: The first language focus is Language pattern 1. Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I go by ___. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 - Listen and point. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
